entry.title: Re: Can Screens be customized in IAS?
entry.content:
Hi @yogananda ,

Can you provide a sample CSS file? I'd like to try it out like this in the Help: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/use-custom-css?q=CSS&version=Cloud 

 

Thanks,

George

entry.title: Re: Material showing in missing part report for productio...
entry.content: The fact that components are available according to ATP check does not mean that the stock is there but that there are receipts planned for a date before the consumption date. Or, some other process can issue these components, unless you established some error message.

entry.title: Re: Material showing in missing part report for productio...
entry.content: Or check if the ATP check rule at orden creation is different from that of release.

entry.title: How To Set Email Notification for PR and PO approval in System.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We need to understand wither email notification is possible in SAP for PR and PO approval or rejection.

If its possible in public cloud then i request you to please share the SAP note.

Thanks

Tapas
PHONE_NUMBER


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How To Set Email Notification for PR and PO approval...
entry.content:
You can find this information in 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ceeb8059ce984943bfe9bbc7e464cd0b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/40c92fe9016a4e6dba80316f01377eef.html

You will find more details in KBA 3304179 - Email notification tab is missing for purchasing workflows.

thanks 

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: New Blog Post Board in the Community Coffee Corner Group
entry.content:
Over the past few months, we have seen an increase in content from many of our community members on topics that are not necessarily related to SAP products, developer topics, or SAP technology. 
A good example of this is the Blog it Forward series, as well as the Monthly Member Spotlight posts from my colleague @marco_v_santos. 

Up until now, this content has all been published in the Welcome Corner group.
The Welcome Corner group is first and foremost there to greet and help new community members, and as we will soon start to publish new help material and tutorials, we decided to create a new blog post board in the Community Coffee Corner group for content not directly related to the community onboarding experience. 

This new blog post board is for everyone, and content published here can be on all topics around the SAP C/community. It doesn't even have to be work-related. 
The Coffee Corner discussion board will of course stay and can be used to ask questions or start discussions with your fellow Community members. 

From the Community Team's side, we will use the new blog post board to celebrate and acknowledge our active contributors, share news about the SAP Champions and SAP Mentors, as well as content related to SAP- and SAP Community events, and much more. Stay tuned! 🙂 

We have already moved all recent Blog it Forward and the Monthly Member Spotlight posts there, so please make sure you subscribe to the Coffee Corner group to be notified of new discussions, blog posts and contests. 

To make it easier for everyone to get to the group, it is now also included in the main Groups navigation under Interest Groups:

entry.title: Re: DRC - No file type for message type TR_RECEIPT_PULL
entry.content: Hi ONBT and Daphne, I am also facing this error in my implementation, can you please confirm how the error was resolved.

entry.title: Creating an SAC story with members from different Hierachy
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am working on creating an SAC story which has members from different hierarchy.

Here, the CF_  accounts (shown in table 1) and table 2 accounts are part of different hierarchy as shown below.

 

 

Currently I have created two different tables for different hierarchy. I want all of them together in a single table.

Can someone please assist me here?

entry.title: RFC Adapter on Edge Integration Cell
entry.content:
Introducing the RFC Adapter on Edge Integration Cell 

 The SAP Integration Suite has taken a significant step forward with the introduction of the RFC receiver adapter on the Edge Integration Cell (EIC) runtime. This enhancement allows seamless migration from PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite, leveraging its diverse capabilities. 

What is RFC? 

Remote Function Call (RFC) is a protocol used to communicate between SAP systems and external applications. It enables the execution of functions in a remote system, facilitating integration and data exchange. 

Key Features of the RFC Adapter 

  • Direct Connection: The RFC adapter now allows direct connection to backend systems via local BTP destinations, eliminating the need for SAP Cloud Connector. 
  • Secure Communication: Secure Network Communication (SNC) is supported, offering authentication, integrity, and confidentiality protection. 
  • Java Connector (JCo) Integration: Utilizes JCo Standalone 3.1 library for transforming remote-enabled Function Module (RFM) calls. 

How it works on Edge Integration Cell runtime? 
SAP Cloud Integration can directly access the virtual systems exposed by the Cloud Connector, via RFC destinations. With this new enhancement, now, Edge Integration Cell allows direct connection to backend systems (without using the SAP Cloud Connector), via local BTP destinations (which can run without internet but needs periodic sync from Cloud) and SNC. Thus, the RFC Sender adapter running on Edge Integration Cell can benefit from native RFC (CPI-C) communication through an SAP Gateway. 

 

The adapter uses the Java Connector (JCo) Standalone 3.1 library to receive and transform remote-enabled Function Module (RFM) calls from SAP or external (JCo-based) systems and applications. RFC Metadata is used for the transformation from native RFC to RFC-XML and vice versa. 

The adapter uses the Java Connector (JCo) Standalone 3.1 library to receive and transform remote-enabled Function Module (RFM) calls from SAP or external (JCo-based) systems and applications. RFC Metadata is used for the transformation from native RFC to RFC-XML and vice versa.  

This blog explains how to use and establish the setup for RFC adapter in EIC runtime. 

Prerequisites for using the RFC Receiver Adapter 

  • The remote systems that RFC adapter connects to can be on-premise or in the cloud. Typically, Cloud Integration can directly access the virtual systems exposed by the Cloud Connector, except for the RFC channel. In Cloud Integration and for its Edge Integration runtime, for the RFC channel it’s necessary to configure a destination in SAP BTP Cockpit.  
    For full help documentation regarding the use of the Destinations Editor (and other methods to maintain destinations), as well as the setup of the different destination types, see SAP BTP Connectivity - Initial Setup. For step by step visual instructions, follow this blog. 
  • Set up SNC on Edge Integration Cell for productive use of the RFC Adapter. For more information, see Set Up SNC on Edge Integration Cell. For step by step visual instructions, follow this blog. 

Prerequisites for using the RFC Sender Adapter 

In addition to the prerequisite mentioned for the receiver adapter, following additional settings are needed: 

  • Create a new destination of TCP/IP type in the ABAP system to establish a connection in the sender system. Note that this prerequisite step is only applicable to ABAP systems and does not apply to non-ABAP systems. For more information, see Setting Up an RFC Destination for the RFC Adapter. 

Why SNC 

RFC connections between SAP Edge Integration Cell and other SAP systems, or third-party systems can be secured with SNC (Secure Network Communication). SNC supports three levels of security protection: 

  • Authentication only 
  • Integrity protection 
  • Confidentiality protection 

To ensure secure communication, Secure Network Communications (SNC) should be enabled in Edge Integration Cell and configured for the RFC adapters. Enabling SNC when using the RFC adapter, especially productively, is strongly recommended. 

Setting up New destination in BTP Cockpit ( Prerequisite) 

  1. For using the adapter in Edge Integration Cell runtime, choose Connectivity > Destinations (New). 
  2. Choose Create. 
  3. Choose From Scratch to create the destinations from scratch and adding all required details like Proxy and Authentication details. Choose From File to import a pre-configured destination. 

    If you choose From Scratch, enter the values in all required fields.
     

     

  4. Enter the details for the parameters <Name>, <URL>, <User>, <Password>, and select <Type> as RFC.

    Note 

    • For setting up an RFC connectivity for ABAP environment using principal propagation. Select OnPremise from the value help and then for <Authentication>, select PrincipalPropagation. 
    • For using RFC adapter in Edge Integration Cell runtime, set Proxy Type as <Local>.
  5. Add the SNC propertied along with the following properties and assign appropriate values: 

    Java Connectivity Parameters (first four properties are mandatory)  

    Parameters 

    Description 

    Connection Parameters 

    jco.client.ashost 

    The IP address or hostname of the SAP application server. 

    Note 

    For runtimes other than Edge Integration Cell, the value provided for application server host (ashost) property is the access control value created in the Cloud Connector. 

    jco.client.client 

    The SAP client number. 

    jco.client.lang 

    The language for communication with SAP SAP (e.g., en for English) 

    jco.client.sysnr 

    The system number of the SAP instance (e.g., 27) 

    jco.client.user 

    The SAP username for authentication 

    jco.client.passwd 

    The password for the SAP user. 

    jco.destination.auth_type 

    While configuring for principal propagation the value for the property is "jco.destination.auth_type = PrincipalPropagation". 

    Connection Pool Parameters 

    (optional) jco.destination.pool_capacity 

    The number of connections that are kept open in the connection pool for reuse. 

    (optional) jco.destination.peak_limit 

    The maximum number of concurrent connections allowed at peak times. 

    WebSocket Connection 

    jco.destination.auth_type 

    If you’re using principal propagation for authentication, for the property jco.destination.auth_type, specify the value as PrincipalPropagation. Don’t specify any additional credentials in the destination. 

    Location ID should correspond to the cloud connector name found in the master instance. 

    jco.client.client 

    The SAP client number 

    jco.client.tls_trust_all 

      

    jco.client.wshost 

    Host for websocket connection 

    jco.client.wsport 

    Port for websocket connection 

    jco.destination.repository_roundtrip_optimization 

    Set the value to 0 that helps you to minimizes the number of roundtrip calls made to the server 

    SNC parameters (Set the following parameters (mandatorily) to use the RFC adapter Edge Integration Cell runtime) 

    jco.client.snc_mode 

    Set the value to 1 to enable SNC. 

    jco.client.snc_partnername 

    SNC partner name. The format for SNC partner names is <p:<distinguished name>, where distinguished name comes from the public certificates of the trusted partners 

    jco.client.snc_qop 

    Protection levels for SNC, which ranges from 1 to 9, where 9 signifies the highest level of protection. By default, the value is 8. 

  6. Choose Create. You can further edit this destination by choosing Edit.  See See: RFC Destinations.

Setting up SNC for RFC adapter on EIC (Prerequisites) 

See Set Up SNC on Edge Integration Cell for step by step instructions on how to configure SNC for RFC adapter.

Adding SNC related properties when you are deploying an edge node solution

For step by step instructions on how to create the Edge Node as a Runtime Location in SAP Integration Suite, see Deploy the Edge Integration Cell Solution. 

Ensure to set following additional properties to enable SNC for the adapter. 

Enable SNC for RFC Adapter 

Choose this option to set up SNC for secure communication over the RFC protocol. It is strongly recommended to enable this option if using the RFC adapter productively. For more information, see Set Up SNC on Edge Integration Cell. 

SNC File System Storage Class (only visible if SNC for RFC Adapter is enabled) 

Enter the storage class for the access mode ReadWriteMany and the binding mode Immediate 

For more information on generating a key pair or uploading an existing one, downloading its public certificate, and importing the public certificates of the SNC partners, see Managing Keystore Entries. 
 
After setting up SNC on Edge Integration Cell, you can now enable and configure SNC communication in the RFC Adapter. See: 

RFC Sender Adapter 

RFC Receiver adapter. 

 

By integrating the RFC adapter into your EIC runtime, you can enhance your system's connectivity and security, paving the way for efficient and secure data exchange. 

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further assistance with the setup. Happy integrating!

entry.title: Live stream your in-person event so anyone in the SAP Community can join
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will share some things to consider if you would like to live stream your in-person event so that community members who aren't able to join the in-person event can still join virtually. What I'm sharing here is what we used to live stream SIT Madrid 2025.

Live streaming the day of the event

First of all, why would you want to live stream your event? Unfortunately, not everyone who is interested in your event will be able to make it in person. Also, space limitations might be an issue. It is not possible to accommodate everyone who is interested in attending. Some might only be interested in a session or two. Well, in those cases, a live stream might help increase the reach of your event. The event can have virtual attendees, and what's great about live streaming is that you can "attend" the sessions any time, as they will be automatically recorded and available on the streaming platform later, e.g. YouTube.

Platforms

  • YouTube account: You can easily create a YouTube account for your event, e.g. https://www.youtube.com/@SIT-Madrid. This will be a new channel, and you will need verify it in order to request access to be able to live stream - https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/2474026?hl=en&ref_topic=9257984&sjid=18308706078969327092-EU. It takes 24 hours before they grant the account access. In the case of SIT Madrid, we have a domain (https://sitmadrid.com) and an email address associated with it, so it was simple to create an account.
    • YouTube chat: Some virtual attendees might want to interact via YouTube chat, so keep an eye on it while you stream.

In the SIT Madrid channel, you'll notice that there are three separate sessions. This is because of a limitation in YouTube that only allows you to livestream for a maximum of two hours. Hence why we create three separate sessions.

  • LinkedIn: Ideally, you will create a page for your event, e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/company/sap-inside-track-madrid/?viewAsMember=true. If you have a LinkedIn page you can live stream on LinkedIn. You can find all details on how to live stream on LinkedIn here - https://www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin/answer/a564446/.

Software

  • OBS Studio is a free and open-source software for video recording and live streaming. If you are not familiar with it, it is crucial to learn the basics. Many tutorials are available on YouTube explaining how to set it up, create scenes, add sources, etc. In the case of SIT Madrid, we used it to customise what we streamed, e.g., adding sponsor logos, showing a background before the start of a session, etc.
    • Multiple RTMP outputs plugin: You are feeling a bit adventurous and would like to stream to multiple platforms, e.g. YouTube and LinkedIn? You can use this plugin to configure multiple RTMPs and stream to all of them simultaneously. See more details here: https://obsproject.com/forum/resources/multiple-rtmp-outputs-plugin.964/
  • Zoom: In order to properly stream the screen of the presenter, every presenter joined a Zoom call. In OBS, we added Zoom as a source, and it was included in the scene. Thanks to @AAncos for sharing his Zoom account :-).

Hardware

  • Laptop: You will need a dedicated computer to run OBS Studio and plug different devices that you will configure in your live stream, e.g. microphone, camera.
  • Microphone: A microphone is needed to properly capture the speaker's voice and include it in the stream. In the case of SIT Madrid, we used the RODE Wireless Go (Gen 2) microphones. The receiver was plugged into the laptop via USB, and we handed the transmitter to the speaker. In our case, we had two transmitters, meaning we could have two speakers presenting and talking simultaneously without having to share the microphone. The transmitters are battery powered so we have to keep charging when not in use.

Speaker with microphone

  • Camera: An external camera focusing on the presenter. This can be as simple as using a mobile phone and connecting it to your computer as a camera. For SIT Madrid, we had a DSLR - Canon EOS R, plugged into a power supply and via USB to the laptop. Most cameras nowadays have accompanying software that lets you use them as webcams.
  • Tripod: That camera has to be fixed to something, right? Either that or someone will need to hold it for quite some time hehe.
  • External battery (optional): If your microphones run out of battery, you can easily charge them while the speaker is using them.

Test your setup

Practice live streaming a few times. I'll say it again... practice before the event a few times. Ideally, you will be using the exact same equipment you are planning to use, and you will test the live stream in the location where the event will take place using the internet connection you plan on using (WiFi/Ethernet). There are many moving pieces and lots of things can go wrong.

Can you do a live stream that isn't accessible publicly? It is possible to do a private or unlisted live stream on YouTube. Not possible on LinkedIn.

Testing live stream the day before the event

In the case of SIT Madrid, we test the entire set-up, including the presenter mic'd up and joining the Zoom call, a few days before the event at the event location.

And that's it... Simple, isn't it? I hope this blog post gives you an idea of what's required to provide a live stream to those in your area who can't make it to the event but can end up watching the recording.

entry.title: Re: S4 call to F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST returning all values even when selecting just one.
entry.content:
As you can see, the display is different so it's not the same code. Production has "OCX" on, probably because using MULTIPLE_CHOICE = ' ', while development has "OCX" off, because using MULTIPLE_CHOICE = 'X'.

Do a debug in the production system.

entry.title: SBPA - Imported project is no longer editable
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have recently moved our SBPA subscription from one subaccount to another, and we only exported a specific version of our project instead of the editable version, so now we have a view only project that we would have to copy or recreate. I'd like to keep the id though. 

Is there a way to make this imported project editable?

Regards,

Gabriela

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
Hi Dan

thanks for your response.
I already re-run the setup on singapore subaccount several times also in different order subsciption/instance. Always subscription creation fails.

Following your advise I successfully creating build process automation objects.

  1. Creating second subaccount in region US
  2. prerequisite adding entitlement 
  3. creating subscription "SAP Build Process Automation"
  4. creating intance "SAP Build Process Automation"

Looking forward seeing you in the codejam

Thanks
Best regards
Jochen

entry.title: Re: Access Control - Assessment of users, roles and their...
entry.content:
Hi RafaMarj,

you're welcome.

To separate existing Legal Units, Locations, etc. by organizational values is best practise and standard because it allows to keep track & control over costs, materials, invoices, salaries, business plans and forecasts, etc. If is something like organizational structure implemented in your ERP, is easy to check. You mentioned ECC SAP system, so if you run t-code SE16 and choose table T001, you'll see all Company Codes created including Company Name/s, address data and other FI values like VAT, Credit Control Area, Fiscal Year variant, Currency, and much more. If is accounting activate, can be seen in table T001B containing Permitted Posting Periods per Company Code. If there is a warehouse with storage location/s defined (virtual, physical, or both), is displayed in table T001L. To check if there are employees at least using HR Organizational Management, choose the table T001P. Plants including details like Sales Organization/s, Purchase Organization/s, shipping point/s are shown in table T001W.

SE16 tables walking conclusion: if these tables are filled by consistent data, is highly possible that same values are used in SAP roles to control an access to these company data. The applied reality is stored in tables AGR_1251, AGR_1252 and other ones.

As the content confuses anyone who is not experienced in SAP Authorizations or pure table data lover, I recommend rather to avoid frustration caused by AGR_* tables and directly ask admin responsible for SAP roles. You mentioned that there should be one, so this is the best way. I can say that if newcomer asks me for an introduction to SAP Authorization Concept and/or SAP roles, I'm pleased to can show off my work and share the knowledge. I think I'm not the only one who loves own job :-))

I can also say that SAP Authorizations admin knows well who is involved in SAP GRC, if GRC installed. And these experts should be able to answer your questions and doubts about compliance and access restrictions for "gonna sold". As SAP roles admins cooperate in most SAP related projects, it would be good for you to meet them and ask. It may help you not only only with this topic but also with future ones.

I wish you the good and success in your new job.

Best regards, Olga

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Build – Q1 2025 Release Highlights
entry.content: Excellent blog. I apologize if this is not the correct place to post this. Is it possible to configure ECLIPSE ADT with OKTA Single Sign On so we can log into SAP systems via Eclipse without knowing passwords? Thank you.

entry.title: Clean core in PP-DS and AATP in S/4HANA PCE: how much is possible?
entry.content:
Hi, I am seeking for some guidance in clean core for PP-DS and AATP in S/4HANA PCE. I was triggered by this blog on EWM and clean core. And was wondering if there are similar considerations for AATP and PPDS in S/4HANA PCE?

It is about the following:

My customer is doing the brownfield conversion from ECC to S/4 RISE with doing "greenfield" PP-DS and AATP in their S/4, moving away from APO. However, they did a lot of developments (140 objects touched and specific heuristics created). Most of them, they are taking them over in S/4 and doing the code remediation. How can we do the code remediation as best possible according to clean core principles? And how much is possible in the areas of PP-DS and AATP (referring to what EWM can only do)

Many thanks for your feedback, 

Kind regards, 

Goedele

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community
entry.content:

Perfectly timed as well, as I need to ramp-up my running in preparation for Hyrox races later this year 😁

entry.title: Auto Goods Issue in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hello Team,

As SAP DM won't support backflush for the component and i'm looking for auto goods issue in SAP DM using Order POD. There are 1000's of components to be consumed automatically once the SFC is started.

Please guide, how this can be achieved?

Do i need to design it in production process design app?

Regards

S A RAHAMAN

entry.title: Re: Item code 'ABC-016' already exists [Message 3504-19]
entry.content:
Good morning!

I was facing this same error recently, when performing these queries we identified that the record existed in tables ITM4, ITM12 and ITM10.

As we are in a QA environment I did the Delete for validation, after deleting these records it was possible to add the item. However, I am still evaluating the possible impacts of this delete within SAP and the correlation of these tables with the item.

Sincerely,
Fábio Almeida

entry.title: SAP CPQ Training by SAP
entry.content: Hi Team, Greetings. I went through the Implementing SAP CPQ, with in learning.sap.com. I am interested in a virtual or class room session to have a good understanding on the topic. Could you pls tell me how to ask or where to find such training. Kr Chakradhar

entry.title: Re: CRM Interactive Script Editor - How to view repositor...
entry.content: Thank for the reply. We have 2 clients in our development system 100 & 300. In 100 it is displayed in sorted order but in 300 it is not. Any reasons how that is possible.

entry.title: Re: Automizing authentication with SAP Datasphere CLI via OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Hi @shelendra!

Thank you for your comment, this is very helpful for me so that I can revise the blog and make it even clearer.
For what you are trying to do, please log in to the CLI first (datasphere login --host <your tenantURL> --secrets-file /path/to/your-secrets.json).
Afterwards you can run the command „datasphere spaces list --host <your tenant URL>“ to retrieve a list of all spaces in your tenant.
You always have to specify the host you want to interact with. Either by adding the "--host <your tenant URL>" option to your command or you can also use the command „datasphere config host set <your tenant URL>“ to set the host globally. Then you can omit the „--host“ option on the following commands, as long as you interact with the defined tenant.

Best regards,

Anton

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Shiroh Kinoshita
entry.content:
Very nice blog @Shiroh44 ! I really enjoyed knowing more about you. I love to learn new cultures, languages and talk to people from different countries and with different realities, it is so inspiring and change the way we see the world.

Keep blogging here, it will be a pleasure to hear more from you.

All the best,

Raquel, from Brazil.

entry.title: BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE GI for Reservation (261) Mat Doc is created but withdrawn quantity is 0
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

When using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE Goods Issue for Reservation (261) Material Document is created but Withdrawn Quantity is 0. It happens only sporadically. The quantity is available in stock.

entry.title: Re: Where can I find learning materials for SAP CPQ concepts, features, and functionality.
entry.content:
Hi @Margit_Wagner, When we search in this link on Learning Journeys and try to find something on SAP Configure Price Quote it takes you to the 22 hours session, which i have already done. But there are more but these are not available or not accessible.

Also I want to do a training with SAP on SAP CPQ, and I cannot find any in the training sessions. Could you pls help. 

KR

Chakradhar

entry.title: Re: Set a field as mandatory (Manage Sales Quotations - V...
entry.content:
The answer could be found here: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rap-cds-annotation-for-read-only-field-only-in-fiori-elements-app/qaq-p/12794722

or here 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rap-cds-annotation-for-read-only-field-only-in-fiori-elements-app/qaq-p/12794722

In my scenario, I need to make a custom field read only on the Sales Order Fiori Screen.

It seems that by creating a new behavior extension on top of behavior definition R_SALESORDERTP, and interface I_SALESORDERTP, and by adding a similar code than the one in the above links "field ( readonly ) YY1_CustomField", the field becomes read only

Let me know if it worked out for you

Maybe if there was a way to modify the involved parties of that enquiry which as far as I can see its not possible as its closed.

For those wondering where COM.ps1 is defined. Stefan pasted its contents in the post:

Tip: Complete Launch Sequence of SAP GUI Scritping... - SAP Community

Which is the second link at the end of this post 😉

entry.title: 1H 2025: Alumni, AI-Assisted Offboarding & Mass Initiation in Onboarding
entry.content:
With the 1H 2025 release, we have delivered new capabilities within SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to streamline onboarding and offboarding processes and improve experiences for new hires, departing employees, and alumni.

Through customer influence on the Customer Community and innovative product enhancements, we are bringing your ideas to life and driving improvements across the recruiting experience.

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinar: 1H 2025 Product Release Webinars. To hear about the roadmap and broader view of our strategy, watch the Global Event Series Strategic Roadmap Updates H1 2025. 

All of the 1H 2025 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 18th, 2025.

In the 1H 2025 release, we have introduced new capabilities to assist organizations with key onboarding and offboarding use cases, including a new alumni experience, AI-assisted offboarding, and mass initiation of onboarding.

Alumni Experience

With this release we are thrilled to launch the alumni experience in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding. The alumni experience gives former employees access to the system after their employment ends, giving them the ability to view and download relevant documents, update personal information, and receive emails post-termination. The alumni experience offers former employees seamless support and mirrors the ease of access they had as an employee.

The alumni experience helps departed employees manage personal information and gives them access to support, documents, and resources. The alumni experience begins when the former employee receives an email with a link to activate their Alumni Access account. From the home page, alumni can view and edit their personal information, biographical information, addresses, contact information, emergency contacts, dependents, and payment information. Alumni also have access to payroll information, including pay statements, payroll and tax-related documents, and tax and compliance-related information such as the W-2 form or the Superannuation form. See a demo of the Alumni Experience.

AI-Assisted Offboarding

 

With this release, AI-assisted offboarding through Joule, SAP’s AI Copilot, helps enable task completion and provides departing employees with a well-structured offboarding experience.

Joule helps improve the offboarding experience for managers, HR teams, and departing employees with support for common offboarding tasks, ensuring compliance with legal requirements and increasing efficiency in the offboarding process.   

Mass Initiation for Onboarding

HR teams and administrators can now leverage mass initiation during the onboarding process with a mass import capabilities, reducing time to hire while improving efficiency and data collection. This update helps facilitate organizational transitions, such as mergers, rehires, acquisitions, restructures and large organizational changes, with ease.  

Learn more about Onboarding release items in these expert-written articles:

  • Key Highlights from the 1H 2025 Release of SuccessFactors Onboarding
  • Alumni Experience – 1H 2025 Release
  • 1H 2025 Onboarding: Enhancements

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our SAP SuccessFactors customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2025 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2025 Release Highlights Brochure 
  • 1H 2025 Release Highlights Video 
  • 1H 2025 Release Highlights News Article
  • Product Release & Road Map Information 

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

Hopefully you got the assistance you needed.  In case you or others have not already found these new blogs, I am posting them here for your review:

SAP ALM API & Integration: Tasks/Defects/Requireme... - SAP Community
Produce a Jira Project Task out of a SAP Cloud ALM... - SAP Community

Hopefully they help.

Else, SAP Consulting is a team you can engage to assist in this area, should you need such assistance.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: 📅 Day 5/30 – Work Centers in SAP PP: The Beating Heart of Production⚙️🏭💡
entry.content:
In SAP PP, Work Centers represent the physical or logical locations where operations are executed — machines, production lines, or even human labor stations. Without them, production is just a plan on paper!

What is a Work Center?
A Work Center contains all the data required to perform, schedule, and cost an operation, including:
🔹 Resource capacity
🔹 Available time
🔹 Cost center assignment
🔹 Activity types (e.g. machine time, labor time)
🔹 Formulas for scheduling and costing
🔹 Default control keys for how tasks should be processed

⚙️ Types of Work Centers
🛠️ Machine – For automated operations (CNC, injection molding, etc.)
👨‍🔧 Labor/Person – Human operators
🏭 Production Line – Flow-based manufacturing
📦 Storage/Packaging – End-of-line handling
📈 How It All Comes Together:
Work Centers are assigned to Routings, which define the operation steps in production. This enables:
✅ Capacity Planning
✅ Detailed Scheduling
✅ Accurate Costing
✅ Pro Tip:

Well-maintained Work Centers = better shop floor control, smoother MRP runs, and accurate production lead times! 🔄📊

💬 What types of Work Centers have you worked with most — manual or automated? Share your experiences below!👇

entry.title: Re: Outbound communication from SAP Public cloud to a Web...
entry.content:
Unfortunately, after discussions with the S/4 Cloud experts, it became clear that the current ABAP systems in the Cloud (S/4 HANA Cloud, Steampunk) do not provide the necessary functionality of setting an HTTP Proxy in a WebSocket-RFC Destination. ☹️  (So Cloud Connector cannot be used atm.)

In a Cloud-based ABAP system, the end user does not have direct access to RFC destinations (transaction SM59), this is "hidden" by these abstract concepts like "Communication System", "Communication Scenario", "Communication Arrangement", etc.

So I guess at the moment, you have only two chances:

  1. The port of the JCo Server needs to be opened on the firewall. Then you can make a direct WebSocket-RFC connection from Steampunk to JCo Server. (No Cloud Connector needed in that case.)

  2. For HTTP connections, the Cloud Connector can be configured in Steampunk. So if you can't open the firewall for security reasons, the other option would be as I outlined somewhere else in this thread:
    - Install a Business Connector (SAP BC) next to the RFC server program, set up the connection to this RFC server and a mapping that converts input/output data from XML/JSon/etc to RFC. ("RFC-XML" could be used by the SAP BC out-of-the-box. Then no mapping is needed.)
    - Expose the SAP BC in the Cloud Connector

    Then the (HTTP) communication can be tunneled through the firewall (from the inside):
    Steampunk --SSL Tunnel--> SCC --HTTP--> SAP BC --WebSocket-RFC--> JCo Server

    BTW: SAP BC is also Java based. So if the JCo server program is only a small wrapper to access a third-party system (like accessing an IBM MQSeries system via IBM's Java libraries), then perhaps this functionality can be moved directly into the SAP BC, and the JCo Server would not be necessary?! (External jar files and custom Java coding can be easily plugged into the Business Connector. That's what it was designed for.) But this really depends on the scenario.

entry.title: Re: We need to call FB03 fiori app with below parameters...

entry.title: alt+tab does not activate SAP cell/page

Good Afternoon All,

I use alt+tab to navigate multiple windows quite extensively.

About 80% of the time when I alt+tab to go to SAP, that page, and/or the cell I was last in, is not engaged, and I have to mouse to re-engage the cell/page.

Just wondering if anyone has solved this issue. I've gone to my internal IT people but they don't have a solution and Google does not seem to have a solution either.

Thank you and have a great day!

entry.title: Re: failed to open the connection, Detail: IM002 [Microso...
entry.content:
Crystal Reports 2025 crashing after selecting the Atlas Mongo Data ODBC source could be due to compatibility issues or incorrect configuration. Here are some potential solutions:

- *Check ODBC Driver Version*: Ensure you're using the correct version (32-bit or 64-bit) of the MongoDB ODBC driver that matches your Crystal Reports installation. You can determine whether your version of Crystal Reports is 32-bit or 64-bit and download the corresponding ODBC driver.
- *Update ODBC Driver*: Try updating the MongoDB ODBC driver to the latest version, as updates often resolve compatibility issues.
- *Configure Access Mode*: Modify the registry settings for Crystal Reports to adjust the ODBC access mode¹:
- *Registry Path*: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Suite XI 4.0\Crystal Reports\Database
- *Create ODBC Key*: Right-click "Database" > New > Key > name it "ODBC"
- *Create AccessMode Value*: Right-click "ODBC" > New > DWORD value > name it "AccessMode" with a value of 0

- *Alternative Solutions*:
- *Use JDBC Driver*: Consider using a JDBC driver for MongoDB, which can be used with Crystal Reports. This involves adding the JAR file to the ClassPath and restarting Crystal Reports.
- *Check Bitness Compatibility*: Ensure the bitness of the Crystal Reports installation and SQL client access installation are the same.²
- *SAP Support*: If none of these solutions work, reach out to SAP support for further assistance, as they may be able to provid

e more specific guidance or patches.³

The error message points to an issue with the `atsql.dll` module, which is part of the MongoDB ODBC driver, suggesting the problem lies with the driver or its interaction with Crystal Reports.⁴

entry.title: Re: Custom Solution Scenarios in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi, vasilii_verkhovskii,

I do not see the ability to create multiple 'Custom' Solution Scenarios.

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: LOOP AT table with a Reference Variable
entry.content:
The answer was given before, but looks like you didn't understand, so I'll try my best here.

You have 3 objects(VAR, NUM, CONNECTION), when you do the append, you are adding their values in CONNECTIONS, so now it has 3 rows, one containing(LH..., then AA..., then KR...) .

In line 49, CONNECTIONS has 3 rows added by each of the objects before, hence, when you LOOP connections it will it will execute get_attributes and out-write 3 times, one for each row of CONNECTION.

When you Loop Connection into VAR you are putting the content of the row into VAR, so at the first pass, var value will be LH-0400, the second will be AA-0017 and the third will be KK-2500. BUT to use get_attributes you are using NUM which has only the value you added before(KK-2500), so you are effectively getting the same value which is in NUM three times.

On your second LOOP you are sending the values of CONNECTIONS into CONNECTION and then using it to get the attributes, so now you are effectively getting the attributes of all rows of CONNECTIONS and writing them after.

I suggest you to debug the execution and see the values of your variables, that will make you understand better what's happening

entry.title: Re: Requirement completion date changes after saving - Cl...

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: LOOP AT table with a Reference Variable
entry.content: Congratulations, excellent explanation, thanks a Lot.

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA supervisior dimension older configuration...
entry.content:
HI @YSE-1, I edited the supervisor dimension and changed it to use employment status as mentioned and ran initial load. What happened is that unmapped now contains employee status + old hierarchy dimension + Ids from the recent select distinct. (update: missed the option of changing it to generated, that did delete the records)

it didn't overwrite anything, instead its appending. they now all exist in unmapped. I tried going into the hierarchy nodes, went 3 levels and deleted one node at leaf level and clicked save. same network issue. Also, If i choose to ignore the unmapped and move one id from unmapped to normal results and try to save it doesn't work either. 

If i go to landing page and filter by supervisor dimension, it errors out.

 

Note, I have attempted creating many custom supervisor dimensions before thinking that maybe the default supervisor dimension got corrupted somehow. Anything I connect to Manager ID ends up with that hierarchy whatever SQL is used.

I will try to delete one single ID at the absolute bottom of a node and save and see if that works. Update: Deleting one by one didn't work either, network error for deleting a single leaf record.

Update 2: I changed the dimension to use company ID and set as generated since I noticed that as an option to try in your message and it seems to have removed them. I'm now changing it back to use select distinct manager ID as a test and to use manager id to connect to and see what happens. Hopefully, that works and only fetches list of ids without the hierarchy, Thank you for all the help.

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA supervisior dimension older configuration...
entry.content: Hi @YSE-1, thank you for the reply, see answer with pictures above.

entry.title: Re: Sequencial test execution
entry.content:
Hi, nedapetrova,

I checked the SAP Road Map Explorer for Cloud ALM and am seeing that "Support of test sequences in test plans" slid and currently is planned Q2 2025, subject to change without notification.

In case there is some specific requirements you have regarding this feature, please be sure to log your request(s) in the Influence Campaign so development can consider them while developing the new feature.

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

There are a few open ideas already that you can review to see if they meet your needs -> then you can upvote them.  The ones I was finding: 

  • Test plan with test sequence: allow to have the same test cases twice in a sequence Request 329381
  • Test Result Attributes for Test Cases and future Test Sequences Request 333461
  • CALM Testmanagement: Test Sequence Request 327426
     
     
    I hope this is helpful to you.
     
    Best regards,
    Paula

entry.title: Re: Minha jornada com ABAP: começando do zero e avançando

entry.title: 🚀 Share Your Best Prompts and Knowledge to Boost Productivity in the SAP Community! 🚀
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

Feel free to share your own prompts or any tips that have helped you in your SAP development journey. Let's collaborate and make our community even stronger!

What to Share:

🖼️ Generative Image Prompts: Have you used any prompts to create visualizations or infographics that helped you understand complex data better?

💡Code Optimization Tips: What scripts or techniques have you used to optimize performance in your SAP projects?

🔧Debugging Assistance: Share your go-to prompts or guides for troubleshooting common errors in SAP applications.

🤖Automation Ideas: Have you developed any workflows or scripts to automate repetitive tasks in SAP? We'd love to hear about them!

📚Learning Resources: What are your top recommendations for SAP Learning Journeys or other resources that have helped you upskill?

Share Your Knowledge:

✍️Blog Contributions: If you've written any blogs or articles that can help SAP Community colleagues, please share them! Your insights and experiences can be incredibly valuable to others in the community.

entry.title: Embed Docusign with Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
We've noticed that in the new Onboarding 2.0 solution, DocuSign is no longer embedded, whereas it is currently embedded and working smoothly in Onboarding 1.0.

DocuSign is a key part of our onboarding process, having it embedded in Onboarding 2.0 would help maintain a seamless experience for our new hires and HR compliance.

Can SAP please look into embedding DocuSign with Onboarding 2.0? 

Thank you,
Stephanie

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding SAP Signature Management by DocuSign, add-on for SAP Customer Experience solutions SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 1.0 Customer Onboarding Services

entry.title: Re: A 25x faster mta.yaml build for SAP CAP Node.js projects
entry.content: Great tip, I used this in a project recently and worked like a charm. I’m wondering why all the official samples all use the npm builder.

entry.title: Re: How to bulk upload data to a custom business object
entry.content:
Hi yujitsumura

The OData service exposure in effect creates an available API. 

So you would need to then call the API from some tool or developed app.

For example, one way would be to create an iFlow using Cloud Integration suite

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/use-csv-to-xml-converter?locale=en-US

You cloud also create a new app with Developer Extensibility that allows mass upload.

Or you could test it with Postman, as mentioned in this Blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/part-viii-custom-business-object-exposure-as-external-web-service-bonus/ba-p/13321117

Here are a couple other links:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/multiple-data-entry-in-custom-business-object-without-odata-service/qaq-p/12801300

As well as this tutorial:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-business-object..html

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: CCM - use multiple condition tables within one condit...
entry.content:
Hi @SD_Jedi, 

Thanks for the detailed explanation—your scenario is standard and should work correctly with proper access sequence setup.

I recommend copying the standard access sequence RE01 and adding your custom condition table (Contract / Material Group). Make sure to activate the "Exclusive" indicator and assign Requirement 75 to the first step. This ensures the system stops searching once a condition is found for the material group and avoids summing multiple rebate percentages. For the fallback step (Contract only), leave the exclusive flag and requirement blank. This configuration should give you the expected behavior: 8% for material group A and 6% otherwise, without doubling values.

If you're still seeing incorrect results, check for any enhancements or custom logic that might be overriding standard pricing behavior.

Best regards,
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: SAP Community AI Conversation Chatbot to ask Q&A?
entry.content:
Do you have some place where we can ask Qs to get quick answers based tons of community questions been answered and accepted as solution... This will benefit for us instead of waiting for answer from community colleagues... SAP Community is having huge knowledge database which can be utilised in a better way so this will increase more non SAP colleagues to join SAP community to get more members .. Do you have vector db for us to use

Any APIs do you plan to release which we can build our own chatbot in Gradio or streamlit

entry.title: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hello! Im trying to get the "Quantity" field on the document lines in a "User defined values" query. 

SELECT $\[$38.11.0]
FROM DUMMY

I've been trying to use the previous query, and after reading through other questions on the subject I've tried:
Removing the backslash
Leaving only the select line
Changing "$38" for "RDR1"
Changing "0" for "number"
Changing "11" for "Quantity"

None of these have worked for me so far, and it either gives me internal error 3006 or syntax error near "$"

I'd appreciate any help on the subject

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch München (06/2025)
entry.content:
Liebe Stammtischschwestern und -brüder,

Wir treffen uns am Donnerstag, den 12.06., zum SAP Stammtisch. Lasst uns in schönem gemütlichen Ambiente zum Thema SAP und drumherum austauschen.

Wir haben diesen Monat als Themenschwerpunkt die neue SAP Sales und Service Cloud v2 mit zwei Experten auf dem Gebiet:

  • Andreas Neumeier (SAP) mit einem kurzen Überblick über die Funktionalitäten
  • Johannes Engelke (Freelance) zum Vorgehen bei Erweiterungen mit side-by-side Extensions

Wir treffen uns TBA. Bitte haltet auch eure Zu- und Absage aktuell, damit wir die richtige Personenzahl für unsere Planung haben.

Wir freuen uns auf Euer kommen und einen regen Austausch!

SAP Stammtisch Orga Team

entry.title: Re: Impact Analysis in Cloud ALM

Hi, Cihan,

Paula

entry.title: Issues with Checkbox Binding in Chart of Accounts Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m working on a customization of the Standard Fiori App: Chart of Accounts and I'm encountering an issue with the behavior of CheckBox controls in different views within an IconTabFilter.

The problem arises because one of the IconTabFilter items uses a Tree View (All accounts), while the other uses a List View(Unassigned Accounts)  Both views contain checkboxes that are bound to the selected property. The values are either "X" (which should be true) or an empty string "" (which should be false). but this is causing a UI bug for the user.

Error:
Error: "X" is of type string, expected boolean for property "selected" of Element sap.m.CheckBox#__box225-__clone1365
Error: "" is of type string, expected boolean for property "selected" of Element sap.m.CheckBox#__box225-__clone136

Additionally, I am seeing the following error in the Console Log:

Oops, the Odata service delivered data in wrong order!

entry.title: SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 patch 15 - URGENT REQUIRMENT
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are in a situation with our SAP HANA update 1909. We are stuck with upgrade and there is no forward path due to the fact we are using SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 patch 14 which is not compatible with 2023 S4HANA and the product is no more supported by SAP. The patch 15 of SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 is compatible with SAP HANA 2023 but there is no way we can get the patch for this product. SAP or SalesForce both do not have the patch 15 and we are literally stuck. We are not able to uninstall this product and CLICK is heavily used by us and we have 2500 field user using it. We understood we need to look into other product like SAP FSM but for us to move forward with S4 HANA 2023 update we need patch 15 of SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3.

Does anyone has patch 15 of this product SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 and if you can find or know someone who can give use the patch 15.

This is urgent and need help.

Regards and thank you.

Arun Ghewarchand

entry.title: Re: Assignment of the type "String" to type "Numeric" typ...
entry.content:
Hi, mahendra_143,

I am going through unanswered questions to try to get a response out to people.  I wonder if you were able to resolve the problem here or perhaps you created a support case to assist you.  If you still need assistance, it might help readers be able to respond if you share detailed steps of what you are doing, where you write the code (share the code) and what is returned on the screen so we might be able to respond to you.

If resolved, feel free to indicate that as well.  (In that case, if you are willing/able, share details of how you solved it in order to help other users in the community who might come across the post.

Best regards,

Paula

entry.title: Watch the SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Highlights Video Series
entry.content:
The SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Highlights videos are now available. These videos showcase exciting self-service innovations for Configuration Manager users, general highlights including exciting AI and Contingent Labor updates that focus on the importance of skills-based hiring, and new Services Procurement enhancements.

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Configuration Manager Highlights
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release General Highlights
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Services Procurement Highlights

You can also watch the full series on the SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone where you’ll also find additional release resources such as the Spotlight Features Guide.

entry.title: BYD2502 Details of summary posting in rev rec run
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,


Our Auditors are asking details of Details: General Ledger Account XXXX posting due to revenue recognition run process ...

when we execute revenue recognition run we get Postings tab with Accounts tab summary of each GL Account posting at summary level and details below in detail tab
Details: General Ledger Account XXXX

But this detail is not enough Auditors are asking all posting to P&L GL Account with details like .. who posted the time how many hours which hours posted as been contributed to revenue recognition run and which were delayed due posting block or any other reasons ..
any time booking cleared or expense cleared or any other direct posting ...

how does SAP works out this summary of GL Posting and which time line has been cleared in revenue recognition run and which were not ... could you please tell us details which can help identify such details ..

SAP has any report which has such details .. we tried revenue recognition run history but no luck ..

we need details of P&L GL Posting with full breakdown of how that cost was initially posted by whom etcs.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 Details of summary posting in rev rec run
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

In SAP Business ByDesign, the Revenue Recognition Run aggregates and posts revenue and cost recognition entries based on the underlying project time confirmations, expense postings, and other cost objects. The auditors' request goes deeper than the default level of detail provided in the Postings tab of the Revenue Recognition Run — they want traceability from the P&L postings back to the original source transactions (e.g., time recordings, expense reports), including user details, dates, hours, reasons for delay/block, etc.

To meet auditor requirements, you'll need to link several reports like (Project Cost and Revenue Details by Project Structure, Journal, Cost Centers - Line Items, G/L Accounts - Line Items...)

You can get the Revenue Recognition Run ID and date, open Journal report for that period and filter by the G/L Account in question, and For each line item, use Reference Document Type and Source Document ID to trace back to Time Recording Confirmation...

The SAP Business ByDesign does not offer a single standard report that provides:

  • Revenue recognition journal entry

  • Original time/expense document

  • User who posted

  • Delay/blocking reason

  • Posting date and clearing info

However, you can build a custom report using Data Sources. Use Business Analytics > Design Reports to combine these into a joined data source/report.

 

Best regards,  
Ihan
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025
entry.content:
Hey @cecilysorenson about the ABAP meets SAP Build Code: some time ago I've tried to configure ABAP in SAP Build Code following the steps of the link below and I got an error. I've replied in the post but I didn't have any answer. Could you tell me any guideline about this configuration? Link: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-up-the-configuration-of-the-abap-cloud-in-sap-build/bc-p/14084904#M180924

Thanks in advance!

Alessandra

entry.title: Re: Adding the Shifts to the Resource/Work Center in the Capacities Tab
entry.content:
@Raja_S_Reddy_B 

The document is very helpful, but it does not fully meet my requirement. I have 4 shifts:

  • Day Shift: 8:00 AM to 5:30 PM (Break: 1 hour)

  • Night Shift: 5:00 PM to 3:00 AM (Break: 1 hour)

  • Friday Shift: 8:00 AM to 5:30 PM (Break: 1.5 hours)

  • Ramzan Shift: 7:00 AM to 2:00 PM (No Break)

When I enter only the Day and Night shifts, the system does not show any error. However, when I also enter the Friday and Ramzan shifts, the system displays the following error:
"Shift finish 17:30:00 is after start of next shift 08:00:00 (Message No. CR146)".

entry.title: Re: Tasks allocated in Cloud ALM for a User that is leaving
entry.content:
Hi, Avani_Anand,
I am going through old, unanswered questions and the community and came across your post.  In case you haven't found this information yet or in case others are wondering similar, I am providing the following information:
The guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/add-deactivate-and-delete-users has the following section that details info on 'deactivated' users and their tasks:

Adding, Deactivating, and Deleting Users

To manage users in the user management of SAP Cloud ALM, the identity of the user has to exist in the identity provider.

In SAP Cloud ALM, the Identity Authentication service assumes the role of the identity provider. This means that business users log on to SAP Cloud ALM with the mechanisms and credentials defined in the Identity Authentication tenant. For more information, see Step 1: Onboard Users in Your Identity Authentication Service. From this follows:

  • Before you can add a user to SAP Cloud ALM, the identity of the user has to exist in the identity provider.

  • Deactivating or deleting a user in SAP Cloud ALM does not affect the user in your identity provider.

For the actions below, the status of the user in SAP Cloud ALM is also important. These are displayed in the table column (Table View), on the user card (Card View), and in the user details.

 

 User Status Description

New

The user has never logged into SAP Cloud ALM. If an approval request is pending for this user, then only Waiting for Approval is displayed as status.

Waiting for Approval

There is at least one approval request pending for this user. To process the request, select Approve or Reject in the Approval Request section of the user details (see Processing Approval Requests).

Active

The user has logged into SAP Cloud ALM, is not deactivated, and there are no open approval requests for this user.

Expiring soon

In SAP Cloud ALM, the Identity Authentication service (IAS) assumes the role of an identity provider. When changing IAS settings in the SAP Business Technology Platform, a grace period prevents users from being automatically deactivated in SAP Cloud ALM and therefore losing personal settings and project assignments. The number of days specified in this status indicates the end of the grace period after which the user will be deactivated. A user with this status is not authorized to use SAP Cloud ALM, but after changing the IAS within the grace period personal settings and project assignments will still be valid.

If you do not want to wait until the end of the period, you can deactivate or delete the user as usual.

Deactivated 

The user has been deactivated due to an administrator action. A deactivated user can still log on, but is blocked for all applications. A user administrator can reactivate the user, but the role assignments are not restored.

 

(I removed some detail here.)

Deactivate User 

A deactivated user is blocked for all applications of SAP Cloud ALM. The corresponding user still exists, but is marked as Deactivated. Also, personal settings are deleted and the user is displayed anonymized.

For auditability reasons, you can still correlate the user to the performed actions if you have the role User Administrator or User Viewer.

Note

The user status Deactivated is needed for traceability; it does not make sense for users who have not logged onto SAP Cloud ALM before. Therefore, you can only deactivate users, who have logged into SAP Cloud ALM before, meaning their status was Active).  

-> In case a user has User Viewer or User Administrator role, they will see the involved deactivated user and other users without these roles will only see "Former Member" (see image taken from mentioned document):

I hope this is helpful.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Unable to create a trial instance in SAP CAL
entry.content:
When trying to deploy the appliance template SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance, after clicking the Accept Terms button, the system generates the following error.

Error

We are sorry, we are not able to provide the requested solution at this time. Try again later or contact the SAP Cloud Appliance Library operators.
 
Can you Assist?
 
Thanks

entry.title: Re: CPF for recurring allowance is calculated for full mo...
entry.content: IT0014 entries generally do not split automatically due to changes in IT0186, we can implement custom logic or configuration adjustments to achieve the desired splitting.

entry.title: Re: CPF for recurring allowance is calculated for full month irrespective of IT0186 change
entry.content: IT0014 entries generally do not split automatically due to changes in IT0186, we can implement custom logic or configuration adjustments to achieve the desired splitting

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Linked defects
entry.content:
Hi, CristinaVasla,

I am going through unanswered questions on the community and came across your post. 

I am not sure if the following will meet your needs or not, but wanted to share it in case you haven't found a solution for your question.  You can see the inline help offers a suggestion that n the case you have a defect that must be handled by multiple people, you can create a sub-task to accommodate this:

In case this won't help your needs, you can always add your idea to the Influence Campaign:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Custom Trust Configuration - Role Collection Mapping
entry.content:
Great explanation, thanks a lot. I have to move the scenario one step further. My CIS is federated with Okta as Corporate Identity Provider. Okta is not sending any groups or attributes, just providing authentication. I would like to assign groups on CIS so I can map them to role collections on BTP. Unfortunately shadow users are created on BTP only, so users do not exist on CIS. If I create manually user on CIS with the same email as in Okta (and shadow user on BTP) authentication on BTP access fails with errors: " error: unexpected response from authentication service" and "status code: 400, reason: invalid_grant, error description: Invalid authorization code: here_comes_some_code". Is such scenario at all possible to achieve?

TIA

BR

Maciej

entry.title: Re: Bootcamp SAP Business Technology Platform | SAP Future Talent | Spanish
entry.content:
Buen día saludos, Excelente! ya estoy invitando a los estudiantes que se unan al evento. 

La actividad tendrá componente teórico / práctico? Recibirán algún comprobante o certificado de participación ?

Quedo atenta. Gracias.

entry.title: Re: Gantt chart disappears when selecting delta lines and...
entry.content:
Hi, former_member135871,

I came across this old post.  In case you are still seeing this issue, please do log a support case for SAP to review and respond/resolve in case it is a problem -> as it appears to be.

Best regards,

Paula

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Defect Statuses and Workflow
entry.content:
Hi,  CristinaVasla,

I am reviewing unanswered questions in the community and trying to get a response to the authors.

There is no standard workflow built into Defects in Cloud ALM. 

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,

Paula

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA supervisior dimension older configuration...
entry.content: Ok, The hierachy has finally been removed if i change the option to generated and replace with a simple dimension -> load -> replace back to what is needed -> load again. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP ME. Can a partner create service requests on beha...
entry.content:
Yes, a partner can create service requests on behalf of a customer in SAP for Me, provided the customer has granted the necessary authorization using the Service Partner User Management application. This allows partners to use their own S-user IDs to create and manage cases on behalf of authorized customers. 

Kindly refer to KBA's below for more information.

Service Partner User Cockpit - SAP for Me
2518736 - Service Partners: How to get authorizations for your S-user ID to create support cases on-behalf of your customers - SAP for Me
2518600 - How to grant support authorization to a service partner S-user using the Service Partner User application - SAP for Me
3575946 - How to use the Copy Authorizations function in SPU Management application – SAP for Me
3594233 - Cloud Administrator is unable to access SPU application due to missing authorization error - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Health Monitoring - Filter ABAP.ResponseTim...
entry.content:
Hi, christoph03,

In Health Monitoring > Configuration for an on-premise (ABAP) system, you can set a Monitoring threshold for Response Time:

Regarding setting a task type filter, I am not sure if I get your meaning.  You can pick and choose what Events you want to send an alert for (based on the defined threshold setting, example see the screen shot above).  Here is an image showing several metrics are set to issue an alert, some with email notification, some with ticket creation:

This setup is done in Configuration cog of the relevant system(s).

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: How to remove the left/right margin?
entry.content:
Hi, 

Is there a way to remove the left/right margin so I can display the entire TreeTable?
